story.hook: Dogs can follow a human point fast.

story.description: A clip of farm animals: a small duck in a bowl of water
story.hook: Ducks reveal the waterproof feathers first.
story.lead: Ducks reveal the waterproof feathers first. Watch the waterproof feathers first, because ducks spread oil from a gland near the tail. That coating helps water bead away, keeping the body warmer and lighter. The pond looks easy because the feather coat is engineered. Would you test it?.

story.description: A clip of farm animals: a duck swimming in the water near a bridge
story.hook: Ducks show why the feathers matters.
story.lead: Watch those webbed feet—most people think ducks kick water like a motor. Not quite. They press down hard, then roll toes forward to squeeze water out. That's why their push looks lazy but moves them fast. Did you see the toes uncurl like that?
story.script: Watch those webbed feet—most people think ducks kick water like a motor. Not quite. They press down hard, then roll toes forward to squeeze water out. That's why their push looks lazy but moves them fast. Did you see the toes uncurl like that?
